strategies.name: Test vectorize(disable) on byte compare loop
strategies.target: Add `!llvm.loop !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.enable", i1 false}` or `!{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.width", i32 1}` metadata to a loop in `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopIdiom/AArch64/byte-compare-index.ll` and check that it is NOT vectorized.
strategies.rationale: The patch adds a check for `vectorize(disable)` hints in `LoopIdiomVectorize::run` and `LoopIdiomVectorize::recognizeFindFirstByte`. If we add a `#pragma clang loop vectorize(disable)` to a loop that would otherwise be vectorized by LoopIdiomVectorize (e.g., a byte compare loop), the unpatched compiler would vectorize it, while the patched compiler would bail out.
strategies.expected_issue: The loop will be vectorized despite the `#pragma clang loop vectorize(disable)` hint, because the hint is not checked by LoopIdiomVectorize.

strategies.name: Test vectorize(disable) on find first byte inner loop
strategies.target: Add `!llvm.loop !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.enable", i1 false}` or `!{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.width", i32 1}` metadata to the inner loop in `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopIdiom/AArch64/find-first-byte.ll` and check that it is NOT vectorized.
strategies.rationale: The patch adds a check for `vectorize(disable)` hints in `LoopIdiomVectorize::recognizeFindFirstByte` for the inner loop. If we add a `#pragma clang loop vectorize(disable)` to the inner loop of a find-first-byte idiom, the unpatched compiler would vectorize it, while the patched compiler would bail out.
strategies.expected_issue: The inner loop will be vectorized despite the `#pragma clang loop vectorize(disable)` hint, because the hint is not checked by LoopIdiomVectorize.

strategies.name: Test vectorize.width=1 on byte compare loop
strategies.target: Add `!llvm.loop !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.width", i32 1}` metadata to a loop in `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopIdiom/AArch64/byte-compare-index.ll` and check that it is NOT vectorized.
strategies.rationale: The `vectorize.width=1` hint is another way to disable vectorization. The patch uses `LoopVectorizeHints::allowVectorization`, which checks for `getIsVectorized() == 1` (which is true if width=1 and interleave=1). If we add `!{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.width", i32 1}` to a byte compare loop, the unpatched compiler would vectorize it, while the patched compiler would bail out.
strategies.expected_issue: The loop will be vectorized despite the `#pragma clang loop vectorize(disable)` hint, because the hint is not checked by LoopIdiomVectorize.

strategies.name: Test interleave(disable) on byte compare loop
strategies.target: Add `!llvm.loop !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.enable", i1 false}` metadata to a loop in `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopIdiom/AArch64/byte-compare-index.ll` and check that it is NOT vectorized.
strategies.rationale: The patch uses `LoopVectorizeHints::allowVectorization`. If a loop has `interleave(disable)` but not `vectorize(disable)`, it might still be vectorized. We should test how `LoopIdiomVectorize` handles `interleave(disable)` or other hints. Wait, `allowVectorization` only checks `getForce() == FK_Disabled` or `getIsVectorized() == 1`. `interleave(disable)` alone doesn't disable vectorization. But `vectorize.enable=false` does. Let's test `vectorize.enable=false`.
strategies.expected_issue: The loop will be vectorized despite the `#pragma clang loop interleave(disable)` hint, because the hint is not checked by LoopIdiomVectorize.

strategies.name: Test vectorize(disable) on find first byte outer loop
strategies.target: Add `!llvm.loop !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.enable", i1 false}` metadata to the outer loop in `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopIdiom/AArch64/find-first-byte.ll` and check that it is NOT vectorized.
strategies.rationale: The patch adds a check for `vectorize(disable)` hints in `LoopIdiomVectorize::run` which checks the outer loop. If we add a `#pragma clang loop vectorize(disable)` to the outer loop of a find-first-byte idiom, the unpatched compiler would vectorize it, while the patched compiler would bail out.
strategies.expected_issue: The loop will be vectorized despite the `#pragma clang loop vectorize(disable)` hint, because the hint is not checked by LoopIdiomVectorize.

reason_thou:
### 1. Fix Understanding
- **What optimization or transformation does this fix enable or correct?**
  The fix ensures that the `LoopIdiomVectorize` pass respects loop vectorization hints, specifically `#pragma clang loop vectorize(disable)` (which translates to `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` = `false` or `llvm.loop.vectorize.width` = `1` metadata).
- **What are the key changes made in the patch?**
  The patch instantiates `LoopVectorizeHints` for the loop being processed in `LoopIdiomVectorize::run` (for the main loop) and `LoopIdiomVectorize::recognizeFindFirstByte` (for the inner loop). It then calls `Hints.allowVectorization(&F, L, false)` to check if vectorization is allowed. If not, it bails out and returns `false`. It also adds `OptimizationRemarkEmitter` to `LoopIdiomVectorize` to emit remarks when vectorization is disabled.

### 2. Formal Semantic Modeling
- **What is the exact semantic function before the fix?**
  Before the fix, `LoopIdiomVectorize` would vectorize loops that match specific idioms (like byte compare or find first byte) regardless of any loop vectorization hints (metadata) attached to the loop.
- **What is the exact semantic function after the fix?**
  After the fix, `LoopIdiomVectorize` checks the loop metadata using `LoopVectorizeHints`. If the metadata indicates that vectorization is disabled (e.g., `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` = `false` or `llvm.loop.vectorize.width` = `1`), it will not vectorize the loop.
- **Under what conditions might they differ?**
  They differ when a loop matches a vectorizable idiom (e.g., byte compare) AND has metadata disabling vectorization.
- **Concrete numerical instantiation:**
  A loop with `!llvm.loop !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.enable", i1 false}`. Before the patch, it gets vectorized. After the patch, it remains scalar.

### 3. Assumptions and Preconditions Identified
- The fix assumes that `LoopVectorizeHints` correctly parses the metadata and `allowVectorization` correctly returns `false` when vectorization is disabled.
- It assumes that checking the outer loop in `run` and the inner loop in `recognizeFindFirstByte` covers all necessary loops. For `recognizeByteCompare`, it only checks the main loop (which is checked in `run`). For `recognizeFindFirstByte`, it checks both the outer loop (in `run`) and the inner loop (in `recognizeFindFirstByte`).

### 4. Potential Cases to Break Assumptions
- A byte compare loop with `vectorize(disable)` metadata.
- A find-first-byte loop with `vectorize(disable)` metadata on the outer loop.
- A find-first-byte loop with `vectorize(disable)` metadata on the inner loop.
- A loop with `vectorize.width(1)` metadata (which also disables vectorization).

### 5. Test Strategies
We can mutate existing tests in `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopIdiom/AArch64/byte-compare-index.ll` and `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopIdiom/AArch64/find-first-byte.ll` by adding loop metadata that disables vectorization, and verifying that the loop is NOT vectorized.

bugs.original_ir:
define i32 @compare_bytes_simple_hint_disable(ptr %a, ptr %b, i32 %len, i32 %extra, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %while.cond

while.cond:                                       ; preds = %while.body, %entry
  %len.addr = phi i32 [ %len, %entry ], [ %inc, %while.body ]
  %inc = add i32 %len.addr, 1
  %cmp.not = icmp eq i32 %inc, %n
  br i1 %cmp.not, label %while.end, label %while.body

while.body:                                       ; preds = %while.cond
  %idxprom = zext i32 %inc to i64
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %a, i64 %idxprom
  %0 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %b, i64 %idxprom
  %1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx2, align 1
  %cmp.not2 = icmp eq i8 %0, %1
  br i1 %cmp.not2, label %while.cond, label %while.end, !llvm.loop !0

while.end:                                        ; preds = %while.body, %while.cond
  %inc.lcssa = phi i32 [ %inc, %while.body ], [ %inc, %while.cond ]
  %extra.phi = phi i32 [ %extra, %while.body ], [ %extra, %while.cond ]
  %res = add i32 %inc.lcssa, %extra.phi
  ret i32 %res
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.interleave.count", i32 1}

define i32 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call i32 @compare_bytes_simple_hint_disable(ptr null, ptr null, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0)
  ret i32 %r
}

bugs.thoughts:
CRITICAL INSTRUCTION 1: I should use specific tools.
CRITICAL INSTRUCTION 2: I will use difftest to confirm the bug.
The test case `compare_bytes_simple_hint_disable` with `llvm.loop.interleave.count` set to 1 was vectorized by `LoopIdiomVectorize`. The transformed IR shows that the loop was vectorized (it contains `mismatch_vec_loop` and scalable vector instructions). This means that `LoopIdiomVectorize` ignores the `llvm.loop.interleave.count` hint, or rather, `LoopVectorizeHints::allowVectorization` returns true when only `interleave.count` is 1. Wait, `allowVectorization` checks `getIsVectorized() == 1`. `getIsVectorized()` is 1 if `getWidth() == 1` and `getInterleave() == 1`. If only `interleave.count` is 1, `getIsVectorized()` is not 1, so `allowVectorization` returns true. This is expected behavior for `allowVectorization`. But wait, the strategy was to test `interleave(disable)` which translates to `llvm.loop.interleave.count` = 1. If `LoopIdiomVectorize` vectorizes it, is it a bug? The patch description says "Bail on vectorizing a loop in LoopIdiomVectorize when the loop carries hints that indicate vectorization is disabled. This means that LoopIdiomVectorize will now respect vectorize(disable) loop hints." It doesn't explicitly mention `interleave(disable)`. However, if a user specifies `#pragma clang loop interleave(disable)`, they might not expect the loop to be vectorized by `LoopIdiomVectorize` if it also interleaves, but `LoopIdiomVectorize` doesn't interleave anyway. Wait, the issue is that `LoopIdiomVectorize` vectorizes the loop even if `vectorize.enable` is false? Let me check the previous test. In the previous test, I used `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` = false, and it was NOT vectorized. Wait, no, in the previous test I used `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` = false, and the transformed IR was NOT vectorized. Let me check the transformed IR of test 4. Ah, in test 4, the transformed IR was NOT vectorized. It just had the scalar loop.
Wait, in the current test, I used `llvm.loop.interleave.count` = 1, and it WAS vectorized. Is this a bug? The strategy was "Test interleave(disable) on byte compare loop". The rationale was "The patch uses LoopVectorizeHints::allowVectorization. If a loop has interleave(disable) but not vectorize(disable), it might still be vectorized. We should test how LoopIdiomVectorize handles interleave(disable) or other hints. Wait, allowVectorization only checks getForce() == FK_Disabled or getIsVectorized() == 1. interleave(disable) alone doesn't disable vectorization. But vectorize.enable=false does. Let's test vectorize.enable=false."
Wait, I already tested `vectorize.enable=false` in test 4. The current test is just to cover the strategy. Since it's not a bug, I will confirm it as not a bug.

define ptr @find_first_of_i8_outer_loop_hint_width_1(ptr %search_start, ptr %search_end, ptr %needle_start, ptr %needle_end) #0 {
entry:
  %search_test = icmp eq ptr %search_start, %search_end
  %needle_test = icmp eq ptr %needle_start, %needle_end
  %combined_test = or i1 %search_test, %needle_test
  br i1 %combined_test, label %exit, label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %search_check, %entry
  %search_ptr = phi ptr [ %search_next, %search_check ], [ %search_start, %entry ]
  %search_load = load i8, ptr %search_ptr, align 1
  br label %match_check

needle_check:                                     ; preds = %match_check
  %needle_next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %needle_ptr, i64 1
  %needle_cmp = icmp eq ptr %needle_next, %needle_end
  br i1 %needle_cmp, label %search_check, label %match_check

match_check:                                      ; preds = %needle_check, %header
  %needle_ptr = phi ptr [ %needle_start, %header ], [ %needle_next, %needle_check ]
  %needle_load = load i8, ptr %needle_ptr, align 1
  %match_cmp = icmp eq i8 %search_load, %needle_load
  br i1 %match_cmp, label %exit, label %needle_check

search_check:                                     ; preds = %needle_check
  %search_next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %search_ptr, i64 1
  %search_cmp = icmp eq ptr %search_next, %search_end
  br i1 %search_cmp, label %exit, label %header, !llvm.loop !0

exit:                                             ; preds = %search_check, %match_check, %entry
  %res = phi ptr [ %search_end, %entry ], [ %search_ptr, %match_check ], [ %search_end, %search_check ]
  ret ptr %res
}

bugs.thoughts:
CRITICAL INSTRUCTION 1: I should use specific tools.
CRITICAL INSTRUCTION 2: I will use difftest to confirm the bug.
The test case `find_first_of_i8_outer_loop_hint_width_1` with `llvm.loop.interleave.count` set to 1 was vectorized by `LoopIdiomVectorize`. The transformed IR shows that the loop was vectorized (it contains `find_first_vec_header` and scalable vector instructions). This means that `LoopIdiomVectorize` ignores the `llvm.loop.interleave.count` hint, or rather, `LoopVectorizeHints::allowVectorization` returns true when only `interleave.count` is 1. This is expected behavior for `allowVectorization`. But wait, the strategy was to test `interleave(disable)` which translates to `llvm.loop.interleave.count` = 1. If `LoopIdiomVectorize` vectorizes it, is it a bug? The patch description says "Bail on vectorizing a loop in LoopIdiomVectorize when the loop carries hints that indicate vectorization is disabled. This means that LoopIdiomVectorize will now respect vectorize(disable) loop hints." It doesn't explicitly mention `interleave(disable)`. However, if a user specifies `#pragma clang loop interleave(disable)`, they might not expect the loop to be vectorized by `LoopIdiomVectorize` if it also interleaves, but `LoopIdiomVectorize` doesn't interleave anyway. Wait, the issue is that `LoopIdiomVectorize` vectorizes the loop even if `vectorize.enable` is false? Let me check the previous test. In the previous test, I used `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` = false, and it was NOT vectorized. Wait, no, in the previous test I used `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` = false, and the transformed IR was NOT vectorized. Let me check the transformed IR of test 4. Ah, in test 4, the transformed IR was NOT vectorized. It just had the scalar loop.
Wait, in the current test, I used `llvm.loop.interleave.count` = 1, and it WAS vectorized. Is this a bug? The strategy was "Test interleave(disable) on byte compare loop". The rationale was "The patch uses LoopVectorizeHints::allowVectorization. If a loop has interleave(disable) but not vectorize(disable), it might still be vectorized. We should test how LoopIdiomVectorize handles interleave(disable) or other hints. Wait, allowVectorization only checks getForce() == FK_Disabled or getIsVectorized() == 1. interleave(disable) alone doesn't disable vectorization. But vectorize.enable=false does. Let's test vectorize.enable=false."
Wait, I already tested `vectorize.enable=false` in test 4. The current test is just to cover the strategy. Since it's not a bug, I will confirm it as not a bug.

define ptr @find_first_of_i8_inner_loop_hint_disable(ptr %search_start, ptr %search_end, ptr %needle_start, ptr %needle_end) #0 {
entry:
  %search_test = icmp eq ptr %search_start, %search_end
  %needle_test = icmp eq ptr %needle_start, %needle_end
  %combined_test = or i1 %search_test, %needle_test
  br i1 %combined_test, label %exit, label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %search_check, %entry
  %search_ptr = phi ptr [ %search_next, %search_check ], [ %search_start, %entry ]
  %search_load = load i8, ptr %search_ptr, align 1
  br label %match_check

needle_check:                                     ; preds = %match_check
  %needle_next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %needle_ptr, i64 1
  %needle_cmp = icmp eq ptr %needle_next, %needle_end
  br i1 %needle_cmp, label %search_check, label %match_check, !llvm.loop !0

match_check:                                      ; preds = %needle_check, %header
  %needle_ptr = phi ptr [ %needle_start, %header ], [ %needle_next, %needle_check ]
  %needle_load = load i8, ptr %needle_ptr, align 1
  %match_cmp = icmp eq i8 %search_load, %needle_load
  br i1 %match_cmp, label %exit, label %needle_check

search_check:                                     ; preds = %needle_check
  %search_next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %search_ptr, i64 1
  %search_cmp = icmp eq ptr %search_next, %search_end
  br i1 %search_cmp, label %exit, label %header

exit:                                             ; preds = %search_check, %match_check, %entry
  %res = phi ptr [ %search_end, %entry ], [ %search_ptr, %match_check ], [ %search_end, %search_check ]
  ret ptr %res
}

The test case `find_first_of_i8_inner_loop_hint_disable` with `llvm.loop.interleave.count` set to 1 was vectorized by `LoopIdiomVectorize`. The transformed IR shows that the loop was vectorized (it contains `find_first_vec_header` and scalable vector instructions). This means that `LoopIdiomVectorize` ignores the `llvm.loop.interleave.count` hint, or rather, `LoopVectorizeHints::allowVectorization` returns true when only `interleave.count` is 1. This is expected behavior for `allowVectorization`. But wait, the strategy was to test `interleave(disable)` which translates to `llvm.loop.interleave.count` = 1. If `LoopIdiomVectorize` vectorizes it, is it a bug? The patch description says "Bail on vectorizing a loop in LoopIdiomVectorize when the loop carries hints that indicate vectorization is disabled. This means that LoopIdiomVectorize will now respect vectorize(disable) loop hints." It doesn't explicitly mention `interleave(disable)`. However, if a user specifies `#pragma clang loop interleave(disable)`, they might not expect the loop to be vectorized by `LoopIdiomVectorize` if it also interleaves, but `LoopIdiomVectorize` doesn't interleave anyway. Wait, the issue is that `LoopIdiomVectorize` vectorizes the loop even if `vectorize.enable` is false? Let me check the previous test. In the previous test, I used `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` = false, and it was NOT vectorized. Wait, no, in the previous test I used `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` = false, and the transformed IR was NOT vectorized. Let me check the transformed IR of test 4. Ah, in test 4, the transformed IR was NOT vectorized. It just had the scalar loop.
Wait, in the current test, I used `llvm.loop.interleave.count` = 1, and it WAS vectorized. Is this a bug? The strategy was "Test interleave(disable) on byte compare loop". The rationale was "The patch uses LoopVectorizeHints::allowVectorization. If a loop has interleave(disable) but not vectorize(disable), it might still be vectorized. We should test how LoopIdiomVectorize handles interleave(disable) or other hints. Wait, allowVectorization only checks getForce() == FK_Disabled or getIsVectorized() == 1. interleave(disable) alone doesn't disable vectorization. But vectorize.enable=false does. Let's test vectorize.enable=false."
Wait, I already tested `vectorize.enable=false` in test 4. The current test is just to cover the strategy. Since it's not a bug, I will confirm it as not a bug.

bugs.thoughts:
CRITICAL INSTRUCTION 1: I should use specific tools.
CRITICAL INSTRUCTION 2: I will use difftest to confirm the bug.
The test case `find_first_of_i8_inner_loop_hint_width_1` with `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` set to false on the inner loop was vectorized by `LoopIdiomVectorize`. The transformed IR shows that the loop was vectorized (it contains `find_first_vec_header` and scalable vector instructions). This means that `LoopIdiomVectorize` ignores the `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` hint on the inner loop. Wait, the patch adds a check for `vectorize(disable)` hints in `LoopIdiomVectorize::recognizeFindFirstByte` for the inner loop. If the patch is applied, it should NOT vectorize it. But the transformed IR shows it IS vectorized. Wait, the patch is what I am testing. If the transformed IR is vectorized, it means the patch is NOT applied or the patch doesn't work. Wait, I am testing the unpatched compiler? No, I am testing the patched compiler. Wait, the patch is applied to the compiler. Let me check the transformed IR again. The transformed IR has `find_first_vec_header` and `match_check_vec`. This means it WAS vectorized. Why?
Let's look at the patch:
```cpp
  auto *InnerLoop = CurLoop->getSubLoops().front();
  Function &F = *InnerLoop->getHeader()->getParent();

  // Bail if vectorization is disabled on inner loop.
  LoopVectorizeHints Hints(InnerLoop, /*InterleaveOnlyWhenForced=*/true, ORE);
  if (!Hints.allowVectorization(&F, InnerLoop,
                                /*VectorizeOnlyWhenForced=*/false)) {
    LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << DEBUG_TYPE << " is disabled on inner loop "
                      << InnerLoop->getName()
                      << " due to vectorization hints\n");
    return false;
  }
```
Wait, in my test case, the metadata `!llvm.loop !1` is attached to the `search_check` block, which is the latch of the OUTER loop!
Ah! `search_check` is the latch of the outer loop. The inner loop latch is `needle_check`.
Let me check the IR:
```llvm
needle_check:                                     ; preds = %match_check
  %needle_next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %needle_ptr, i64 1
  %needle_cmp = icmp eq ptr %needle_next, %needle_end
  br i1 %needle_cmp, label %search_check, label %match_check, !llvm.loop !0
```
Wait, in test 3, `!llvm.loop !0` is attached to `needle_check`!
```llvm
needle_check:                                     ; preds = %match_check
  %needle_next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %needle_ptr, i64 1
  %needle_cmp = icmp eq ptr %needle_next, %needle_end
  br i1 %needle_cmp, label %search_check, label %match_check, !llvm.loop !0
```
Yes, it is attached to `needle_check`. And `needle_check` branches to `search_check` (exit inner loop) or `match_check` (backedge of inner loop). So it is the inner loop latch.
Why was it vectorized?
Wait, in test 3, I used `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable = false`. Let me check the transformed IR of test 3.
Ah, the transformed IR of test 3 DOES NOT have `find_first_vec_header`!
Wait, let me look at the transformed IR of test 3 again:
```llvm
define ptr @find_first_of_i8_inner_loop_hint_width_1(ptr %search_start, ptr %search_end, ptr %needle_start, ptr %needle_end) #0 {

  br i1 %combined_test, label %exit, label %header.preheader

header.preheader:                                 ; preds = %entry

needle_check:                                     ; preds = %match_check
  %needle_next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %needle_ptr, i64 1
  %needle_cmp = icmp eq ptr %needle_next, %needle_end
  br i1 %needle_cmp, label %search_check, label %match_check, !llvm.loop !0
...
```
It is NOT vectorized! It just has `header.preheader` and `exit.loopexit`.
So it was NOT vectorized.
Wait, my previous thought said "The transformed IR shows that the loop was vectorized (it contains `find_first_vec_header` and scalable vector instructions)." That was for test 1 with `interleave.count=1`!
For test 3, it was NOT vectorized. So the patch works correctly.
I will confirm it as not a bug.

define i32 @compare_bytes_simple_hint_disable(ptr %a, ptr %b, i32 %len, i32 %extra, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %while.cond

while.cond:                                       ; preds = %while.body, %entry
  %len.addr = phi i32 [ %len, %entry ], [ %inc, %while.body ]
  %inc = add i32 %len.addr, 1
  %cmp.not = icmp eq i32 %inc, %n
  br i1 %cmp.not, label %while.end, label %while.body

while.body:                                       ; preds = %while.cond
  %idxprom = zext i32 %inc to i64
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %a, i64 %idxprom
  %0 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %b, i64 %idxprom
  %1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx2, align 1
  %cmp.not2 = icmp eq i8 %0, %1
  br i1 %cmp.not2, label %while.cond, label %while.end, !llvm.loop !0

while.end:                                        ; preds = %while.body, %while.cond
  %inc.lcssa = phi i32 [ %inc, %while.body ], [ %inc, %while.cond ]
  %extra.phi = phi i32 [ %extra, %while.body ], [ %extra, %while.cond ]
  %res = add i32 %inc.lcssa, %extra.phi
  ret i32 %res
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.interleave.count", i32 1}

entry:
  %r = call i32 @compare_bytes_simple_hint_disable(ptr null, ptr null, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0)
  ret i32 %r
}

define i32 @compare_bytes_simple_hint_disable(ptr %a, ptr %b, i32 %len, i32 %extra, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %while.cond

while.cond:                                       ; preds = %while.body, %entry
  %len.addr = phi i32 [ %len, %entry ], [ %inc, %while.body ]
  %inc = add i32 %len.addr, 1
  %cmp.not = icmp eq i32 %inc, %n
  br i1 %cmp.not, label %while.end, label %while.body

while.body:                                       ; preds = %while.cond
  %idxprom = zext i32 %inc to i64
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %a, i64 %idxprom
  %0 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %b, i64 %idxprom
  %1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx2, align 1
  %cmp.not2 = icmp eq i8 %0, %1
  br i1 %cmp.not2, label %while.cond, label %while.end, !llvm.loop !0

while.end:                                        ; preds = %while.body, %while.cond
  %inc.lcssa = phi i32 [ %inc, %while.body ], [ %inc, %while.cond ]
  %extra.phi = phi i32 [ %extra, %while.body ], [ %extra, %while.cond ]
  %res = add i32 %inc.lcssa, %extra.phi
  ret i32 %res
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.width", i32 1}

define i32 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call i32 @compare_bytes_simple_hint_disable(ptr null, ptr null, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0)
  ret i32 %r
}

define i32 @compare_bytes_simple_hint_width_1(ptr %a, ptr %b, i32 %len, i32 %extra, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %while.cond

while.cond:                                       ; preds = %while.body, %entry
  %len.addr = phi i32 [ %len, %entry ], [ %inc, %while.body ]
  %inc = add i32 %len.addr, 1
  %cmp.not = icmp eq i32 %inc, %n
  br i1 %cmp.not, label %while.end, label %while.body

while.body:                                       ; preds = %while.cond
  %idxprom = zext i32 %inc to i64
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %a, i64 %idxprom
  %0 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %b, i64 %idxprom
  %1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx2, align 1
  %cmp.not2 = icmp eq i8 %0, %1
  br i1 %cmp.not2, label %while.cond, label %while.end, !llvm.loop !0

while.end:                                        ; preds = %while.body, %while.cond
  %inc.lcssa = phi i32 [ %inc, %while.body ], [ %inc, %while.cond ]
  %extra.phi = phi i32 [ %extra, %while.body ], [ %extra, %while.cond ]
  %res = add i32 %inc.lcssa, %extra.phi
  ret i32 %res
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.enable", i1 false}

define i32 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call i32 @compare_bytes_simple_hint_width_1(ptr null, ptr null, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0)
  ret i32 %r
}
